findings.title: Unbounded memcpy into fixed 8-byte heap buffer in parse_alpha
findings.description: parse_alpha allocates 8 bytes then memcpy's `claimed` bytes from attacker-controlled input without bounds check.

findings.title: malloc(8) too small for input in parse_alpha
findings.description: Same root cause as f001 — fixed-size allocation followed by input-length copy. Duplicate report from a different angle.

findings.title: Unbounded memcpy into fixed stack buffer in parse_bravo
findings.description: parse_bravo copies `len` bytes into a fixed-size stack array without bounds check.

findings.title: fread buffer used as randomness source
findings.description: Claims the 4096-byte read buffer is used for cryptographic randomness. It's a file-read buffer; no randomness consumer exists.

findings.title: Possible null deref if fopen fails
findings.description: Claims fread(buf, ...) derefs null f. There is an explicit `if (!f) return 1;` guard on the preceding line.
